mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which typically weakens consumer spending and corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate exceeding 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust economic activity, which strengthens corporate earnings and drives equity valuations higher, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect's Rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal higher discount rates that reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns remain negative despite a 2% increase in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates stronger consumer confidence and demand for goods/services, which drives equity market optimism and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns remain negative despite a 5% increase in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns remain negative despite a 100k+ increase in PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect's Rank IC drops below 0.05.
